summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on May 24, 2022. The board accepted several Police Week donations including gift cards from Texas Roadhouse, LaRosa's via CP AAA, and from Jimmy and Donna Ropsick. Personnel matters were addressed including resignations of part-time firefighters and a volunteer termination, and after an executive session the board voted to terminate a probationary full-time firefighter paramedic. Administrator Pegram discussed a recent successful Township Association meeting with 30 community leaders and announced plans for regular meetings among administrators of the five largest Clermont County townships.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (May 11–May 24, 2022) – passed
Motion to accept 23 $10 Texas Roadhouse gift cards from Milford Texas Roadhouse – passed
Motion to accept four $40 LaRosa's gift cards from CP AAA – passed
Motion to accept a $100 LaRosa's gift card from Jimmy and Donna Ropsick – passed
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter Slate – passed
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Matt Traver – passed
Motion to terminate volunteer Tyler Fritz effective 5-20-2022 due to inability to obtain firefighter certification – passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:13 PM – passed
Motion to terminate a probationary full-time firefighter paramedic effective May 24, 2022 at 0700 – passed
Motion to adjourn at 8:57 PM – passed
action_items: Donna to forward the resolution to all three trustees for vote at the May 28th meeting
Administrator Pegram to attend first meeting with administrators of five largest Clermont County townships on Friday
Fire department to conduct training burn on 132 North on May 25, 2022
key_amounts: $230 total in Texas Roadhouse gift cards (23 x $10) donated by Milford Texas Roadhouse
$160 total in LaRosa's gift cards (4 x $40) donated by CP AAA
$100 LaRosa's gift card donated by Jimmy and Donna Ropsick
175,000 of 200,000 Clermont County residents represented by the five largest townships
notable_topics: Fire department personnel changes – resignations and termination of volunteer
Executive session regarding public employee discipline/termination
Termination of probationary full-time firefighter paramedic
Township Association meeting recap with 30 community leaders
New initiative for five largest Clermont County township administrators to meet regularly
Trash cleanup on a Township road
Resolution from Township Association needing board vote
notes: Executive session lasted approximately 1 hour and 42 minutes (7:13 PM to 8:55 PM), which is notably long. The terminated probationary firefighter paramedic's name was not stated in the public session. The transcript has significant portions of garbled/repeated text likely from automated captioning issues. A firefighter named Slate resigned due to overtime commitments at his career department. Matt Traver resigned to attend paramedic school. Tyler Fritz was terminated for failing to obtain firefighter certification.
title: BOT MTG 5.24.2022.mp4
